subject: Chemistry
title: Aristotle's Four Elements
description: Learn Aristotle's Four Elements in one minute.

hook.text: For 2,000 years, people believed everything was made of just four things: earth, water, air, and fire.

buildup.text: Aristotle taught that mixing these four in different ratios created all matter. Scholars agreed.

discovery.text: Robert Boyle challenged this in 1661. He said an element is a substance that can't be broken down further.

twist.text: Boyle was right, but he still believed in alchemy and spent years trying to turn lead into gold.

climax.text: His new definition launched modern chemistry. Elements aren't mystical—they're testable.

punchline.text: Four ancient elements became over 100 real ones.

quiz.question: Who first defined an element as a substance that cannot be broken down further?
